title: Policy Applicability Cannot Be Reconstructed
scenario: A clinical action references historical policy evidence, but the system cannot reconstruct which policy governed execution at the relevant boundary.
initial_state: Historical policy artifacts indicate the action may have been permitted earlier.
state_transition: Policy applicability evidence is missing or inconsistent during execution evaluation.
execution_boundary: The clinical action is evaluated before relying on historical policy evidence.
constitutional_evaluation: Execution requires reconstructable evidence of the currently applicable governing policy.
constitutional_basis: Execution is inadmissible because policy applicability cannot be reconstructed.
proof_conclusion: Clinical execution must fail closed when governing policy applicability cannot be reconstructed.
clinical_action: Execute action with unreconstructable policy basis
